_sources.ovx: CBOE CDN OVX_History.csv - D0 uses Feb 27 close (Feb 28 = Saturday); D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend, market closed); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.vxeem: CBOE CDN VXEEM_History.csv - all D0-D9 confirmed from live data; D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.scenarios: D10-D12 confirmed from banker-conflict.json; all prior days estimated from narrative context

days.note: Mon - Iran drone strikes Kuwait power & desalination plant; Indian worker killed (Al Jazeera, CNBC, The National); Trump says US wants to 'take Iran oil' / seize Kharg Island; posts 'big day in Iran'; Pentagon weighs ground invasion (CNBC, Al Jazeera, NBC, Axios); US-Israel hits Tehran power grid (temporary blackout then restored); Trump claims Iran agreed to 'most of 15 points' but Iran contradicts; Brent $115.93 confirmed (+3.0%, Al Jazeera/oilpriceapi) - record monthly surge >62% from pre-war; tail risk raised to 43%

Wed - Trump declares US 'leaving Iran in 2-3 weeks'; White House prime-time address 9pm ET; Brent -13.2% to $100.64 confirmed (Reuters Apr 1); WTI $98.04 confirmed (Reuters); Iran simultaneously fires 7 missiles at Israel (cluster munitions, 16 injured, Jerusalem Post) + drones Kuwait Airport fuel tanks (KUNA); IRGC threatens 18 US tech firms deadline 8pm Tehran time (CNBC); Houthis claim third operation vs Israel; tail risk lowered to 37%

Wed - Trump vows 'extremely hard' strikes for 2-3 weeks, 'bring to Stone Ages' (Axios/NBC/CNBC Apr 2) - complete reversal of Apr 1 'leaving' signal; Brent surges from $100.64 to ~$109; Iran launches massive attacks on UAE (457 ballistic missiles + 2,038 drones intercepted, 11 dead, 191 injured), Israel (4 barrages), Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait; Philippines signs bilateral toll-free Hormuz deal with Iran (Bloomberg, The National); Dated Brent physical $141.37 (Bloomberg) - highest since 2008; tail risk raised to 50%

Thu - April 6 deadline 3 days away; Brent $109.03 confirmed (Gulf News/CNBC, +8.3% from D31); WTI ~$111/bbl live (OilPriceAPI NYMEX); TTF €49.97 (oilpriceapi Apr 2 16:03 UTC); JKM ~$20.13/MMBtu (Investing.com Apr 2); Gold $4,675 (Fortune Apr 2); Iran denies negotiations ('trust level zero'); Philippines bilateral deal in effect; tail risk 50%

Fri - US F-15E shot down over Iran (first US aircraft lost in conflict); 1st crew member rescued same day (CNN, NBCNews Apr 3-4); A-10 Warthog also hit near Hormuz, pilot ejected and rescued (CBS); Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum (Al Jazeera Apr 4): open Hormuz or face 'hell'; AIS transits down 95% per S&P Global early April; Brent ~$110 (est., deadline anxiety)

Sat - 2nd F-15E crew member rescued deep inside Iran ('Easter miracle' - Trump, CBS/NBC Apr 5); Kuwait power and water desalination plant taken offline by Iran attack, 90% drinking water at risk (Fortune Apr 5, Al Jazeera); Trump threatens to strike power plants AND bridges if Hormuz not open by Apr 6 8pm ET; Iran: 'not until fully compensated'; Gold ~$4,635 (150currency.com Apr 5)

Mon - April 6 8pm ET deadline active; Brent ~$110.50 est. (CNBC +1.4% deadline anxiety); WTI $111.81 confirmed (OilPriceAPI Apr 6); TTF ~€52.40 est.; JKM ~$21.00 est.; Kuwait desalination offline; Iran refuses - 'not until fully compensated'; AIS transits -95% from historical avg (S&P Global); iTraxx Asia IG ~195bp est. (+7bp); tail risk 54%

Tue - Apr 7 8pm ET deadline (ISW: extended from Apr 6); Iranian strike injures 15 US soldiers in Kuwait (J.Post Apr 6) - first confirmed US military casualties on Gulf soil; Iran pushes peace proposal, Trump rejects: 'not good enough, Tuesday deadline final' (J.Post, Al Jazeera Apr 6); IDF strikes Iran's largest military facility (J.Post Apr 6 live); Brent ~$111 est. 'near four-year highs' (CNBC TV18 Apr 7 early Asia); Gold ~$4,685 est. (Sunday Guardian Apr 7); tail risk 56%

Wed - Apr 7 deadline passes into limited US-Israel strikes on Iranian grid and bridge targets; Tehran Toll Booth remains active; Qatari LNG restrictions persist; AIS transits remain about 95% below normal; Brent ~$118.20, JKM ~$23.60, iTraxx Asia IG ~206bp; tail risk 60%

Thu - Trump announces conditional 2-week US-Iran ceasefire (Apr 8); Brent crashes to ~$97/bbl (-18%), TTF plunges ~15% to ~€45/MWh; Iran claims Hormuz still closed (Israeli Lebanon strikes); VP Vance leads Iran talks in Pakistan Apr 12; tail risk 30%

Fri - Brent $95.20 (-0.75%, TradingEconomics); TTF €44.46/MWh (OilPriceAPI); JKM $19.42/MMBtu (-0.38%, TradingEconomics); Hormuz ~4 transits/day (S&P Global); ADNOC CEO: strait not open despite ceasefire (Al Jazeera); Trump: Iran 'dishonorable' on Hormuz; US-Iran delegations fly to Islamabad; tail risk 32%

Sat - market closed; USS Frank E Peterson + USS Michael Murphy transit Hormuz east-to-west for mine-clearing — first US warship passage since Feb 28 (CBS News, Al Jazeera); Iran: 'This is the last warning' to US warships (Fortune); 3 oil supertankers (2 Chinese + 1 Greek) transit Hormuz (Fortune); Vance-Iran Pakistan talks begin in Islamabad (ABC News, CNN); tail risk 35%

Sun - market closed; Vance-Iran Pakistan talks end: NO DEAL after 21 hours (NBC News, CNBC); sticking points: Iran nuclear commitment + Hormuz sovereignty; Iran (IRIB): 'excessive demands by America prevented any agreement'; Vance: 'we haven't seen that yet' on nuclear commitment; talks to continue Sunday; tail risk 38%

Mon - Trump orders US naval blockade of Hormuz after talks fail (CNBC, NBC, CNN, NPR Apr 12-13); Iran: 'Strait of Hormuz will not be opened'; IRGC warns of 'regretful response' to US warships; Brent +7% to ~$102 est.; Netanyahu signals Israel-Lebanon talks Apr 14; tail risk 48%
